text: aita for changing my diet ? context: i’m 20 yr old — i’m currently home from college for the summer. i was at school for 6 months and lost a lot of weight from a lifestyle change. i live with my parents whenever i’m home from school; i’m leaving again in about a month. ever since i’ve lost weight, my family (who, for context’s sake, is all overweight and wants to lose weight themselves actually) limbos between outright harassing me (ex: random invasive “hugs” that are actually ways for them to touch my body to see if i’m wearing any spanx or anything like that, interrupting conversations to comment on my body out loud, etc) and making fun of me for eating properly. for example, my aunt hired a trainer to help her and my cousin lose weight and she only ever strictly follows those rules without any breaks— i just sort of watch what i eat and eat what i want in moderation & she, for some reason, is jealous of that even though i’m 30+ years younger than her, so she makes snide comments about my food. my mother has started to do the same. when i’m at home now, if i’m weighing my food so that it’s a suitable portion, she makes fun of me for that. and this morning, she made fun of me for literally making a fruit salad to take to work with me for lunch. she started making comments about if it were enough or not, implying that i’m anorexic which is just... really inappropriate and insensitive. the whole 9 yards. so, having been sick of being poked at for the past few months, i got somewhat snippy with her and said “so when i was fat it was cool to refer to me binging on mcdonald’s as “fueling my body” and not saying anything at all about me actually ruining my health but now that i care about myself it’s a problem?” and i feel bad for snapping like that because i get to them these are probably hilarious jokes but it’s getting incredibly old. i called this post that because idek how to frame the actual question but: am i the asshole? not trying to shame anyone of any size who enjoys mcdonald’s, there’s nothing wrong with that and i’m not the health police. i just literally did used to binge
label: NTA

"we'll talk about it when i get home." when i get home my wife basically says "well rats are social animals and looking after two rats is pretty much the same as looking after one. they'll live in the same cage." "just don't bother me about the rats or ask me to do anything

text: wibta for wanting to move new fence inside property line after neighbor won’t share cost? we moved in 4 years ago, our house has really old split rail fence that we knew we’d have to eventually replace. a year and a half ago, we got a neighbor who asked if they could tie into our fence and being neighborly, we said “sure! but we’re going to have to replace it in the next few years”. they have 3 dogs. well the time has come. i got quotes for aluminum and split rail. the aluminum is 11k, split rail 8k. since aluminum is permanent we decided to go with the aluminum. we asked the neighbor if they’d chip in to help pay for the fence (since they use it). we said “even if they’d give us their share of the split rail, we’d be grateful”. their share of the cheaper fence is 1k. they came back and said that they spoke to the hoa and they’re not required to pay for any of the fence. we said, “correct. we didn’t expect anything but we just wanted to ask”. aita if i say fuck it, move the fence in a foot into our property line and they can build their own fence? i don’t want to start a neighbor war but if roles were reversed we would give them our fair share. also i’m petty as hell so i have that working against me. flip side, can anyone explain their point of view for me? i just can’t wrap my head around it.

text: aita [30m] for giving a child [10f] a $20 bill at her birthday party? with corona going around, this has encouraged the neighbors on my street to get better acquainted. one neighbor invited me to his daughters 10th birthday party. i forgot about the party, but only remembered because it was on their front lawn. i totally forgot to get the girl anything. i still went over. i saw the birthday girl, pulled a 20 out of my pocket and handed it to her while saying happy birthday. well, another neighbor told me a few were talking and think it was inappropriate to give a child cash. i should have had it in a card, or given it to her parents i was annoyed because when i was 10, someone giving me 20 bucks would be the highlight of my year. aita for handing her cash? should it have gone through the parents? edit to add: the real reason for me asking reddit, was the neighbor insinuating me as an adult male should not give an underage child, especially a girl, cash in hand. it looks creepy. i'm really asking am a creep?

text: aita - kids college - divorced dad aita here? been divorced 14 years, paid full child support the entire time plus my share of medical bills, etc. have twins starting college next year, and my ex is asking what i can contribute financially. she makes exponentially more than me, and while i have no issues with how she spends her (or the child support), they do take extravagant vacations each year (bahamas, jamaica, hawaii, etc.), have a maid, both kids drive later model suv's, etc. in contrast, i have an extremely tight budget. i have not been able to save for my own financial future. my plan was, once support was over, is to start max'ing out my 401k and build up my savings account. my ex is wanting me to continue to pay the same amount of child support, but directly to the kids, while they're in college. i have communicated that i'm not in a position to help with a set value each month, but if there are expenses i can help with, or just send them some spending money, i'm good with that, or if they want to go to school near me, they can live with me rent free and i'll support them that way. i was in the military, so i had the gi bill, plus i worked full time all thru college, with no financial help other than the us taxpayer :-) my feeling is that maybe she should have put some of that child support away for college, or maybe not taken $5-$10k trips every year. if was sitting on a pile of cash, then by all means, i'd be more than happy to help! i feel at this stage in my life, i need to start planning for my own financial security, and help out in other ways, and to provide cash when i'm able. so aita for not continuing to pay to support them while in college?
label: NTA

raise because it was a very exceptional thing considering the pandemic and the budget cuts that come with it, and all. i didn’t think much of it because i was under a lot of stress (me applying for raise, since she has slightly bigger responsibilities

text: aita for ruining someone's slumber party? my son, 7m has been dancing since he was ten months old. my wife is a dancing hobbiest, and she signed up for a mommy and me ballet class when he was a baby. he never stopped and is now really good. he currently takes classes at a local ballet studio. a mom of one of the other students in my son's class decided she wanted to host a slumber party after the last performance of the school year. this idea was very popular. when she emailed invites, she invited every single student except for my son. she even invited a little girl that quit halfway through the year! i emailed her asking if she made a mistake, and she said she didn't want a boy at her sleepover. this really upset me. my son loves dancing! all of his best friends are in this class. everyone will be talking about this party that he can't go to. my heart broke for him. i talked to my wife, who agreed this was bs, but also said we shouldn't do anything to cause drama and be "those parents." i tried to put it from my mind, but i couldn't. so i emailed all of the other parents, explaining how hurtful it is for kids to be excluded. many responded, agreeing that my son should be invited because he's part of the class. a bunch of nasty debates broke out in the email chain. my wife was annoyed with me at first, but then she got pissed at the other mom when she read her emails. other mom said i was displaying "typical male entitlement" and that it was gross i was teaching my son to "force his way into women's safe spaces." a bunch of people jumped on these comments and said she was being nasty about a little kid. another mom decided to have a slumber party that night as well (that my son is invited to) and now most of the class is going to that one, and only three girls (plus her daughter) will be at the original party. the mom who had the original idea and her friends have sent me some nasty messages. my wife is pissed at her, but also at me, because she says i didn't think before i burned bridges. i don't want a bridge with this
label: NTA

text: aita for trading my dads childhood jeep for a motorcycle? my dad bought a 1995 red cherokee 2 door jeep when he was in his 20’s. he bought it for scraps and fixed it up and got it running. he used that car for years after my mother and him got divorced. it’s old now, well over 200k miles. he gave it to me last year for taking care of his lawn all summer while he was away. i mowed his lawn all summer and worked on his sprinkler system, etc. at the end of the summer he gave me the keys. fast forward to today, i have used the jeep for the last year but it is a gas guzzler. it gets 15mpg. it’s rusting and just not running very well. i went on craig’s list and saw someone had listed a 2014 honda shadow for trade for a jeep cherokee. i sent pictures of the jeep over and did the trade, the motorcycle is gorgeous... i can’t believe i swung it. it’s in pristine condition. after not seeing the jeep for a week or two, he asked me if everything was okay and i told him i traded it. he blew up. said i was selfish and he gave it to me as a gift and that i should have talked to him first. i felt like i had earned it for working for him and could do with it what i like. aita here?

text: aita for kicking out my future daughter in law potentially making her homeless? when the pandemic hit, my husband and i asked my son (25m) and his girlfriend (24f) to stay with us and our both stay with us, as we didn't know how travel would work at the time, but she still seemed hesitant. however, they did end up moving in. everyone had always gotten along quite well, so we had no she is a very picky eater and either makes her own food or picks at what i cook. then i notice her staying up late into the night eating. she did often replace the food, but then she'll eat the replacement food too, and my daughter, who is still finishing classes online found it extremely disruptive, especially as she has had anorexia in the past, and constantly having no access to certain snacks made me worried she would relapse. i asked her gently if she would move out, but that if she wanted to move to a nearby apartment, she was still welcome to come by anytime. she agreed to move out, but my son informed me that she was really panicked because she didn't really have anywhere to go, as they moved a couple states to stay with us, and since she can't travel to her parent's country right now and doesn't have much money to find a place due to being unemployed, she was staying in an airbnb while trying to figure things out. i do feel badly, as
label: YTA

text: aita for refusing to go to my mom's house? i recently started going to a therapist and he's been trying to get me to say what i feel, express emotions and think more about myself. i have always had extreme trouble with these things, to the point where i don't even know what emotions i'm feeling because i've always hid them instead. it started with me being late to my new job. i'm 17 and it's my first "real" job so it's a big deal for me. i realised on the bus that i was going to be late, so i called my mom for advice. she flipped, but came to the rescue. during the ride she was yelling to me about how irresponsible i was (which is true). i had a panic attack, because i felt like such a failure and a burden to everyone. she told me to stop (you can't just stop a panic attack) until i yelled at her she was making it worse. after many attempts i could almost control my breathing again but i could tell she got hurt and i feel so bad about yelling at her. another day i was on my way to work again and she told me to clean my room. i understood it as cleaning my room but she meant just picking up clothes or something. she had been in my room picking at stuff that morning and i asked her to stop. since i was asleep 2 minutes ago i wasn't very patient so when she refused i raised my voice and told her to get out (very unnecessary). she texted me while she was downstairs that if i didn't clean, she would. i don't have anything to hide in my room, but she knows that i hate when she is in my room. i told her then and there that i would not come to hers next week if she would threaten me like that. she told me she would drive me to my dad's directly after work. when we were in the car she asked if i could "explain my little tantrum". i told her my perspective and she said i "painted her as a villan but if that's what i wanted then go ahead". i don't really remember what she said because i tend to shut down when people yell at me but at
label: ESH

text: aita for buying my sister a dress for my wedding? my sister is non-binary (but does prefer term sister over sibling) and is my maid of honor. they often don’t mind wearing feminine clothes so i asked if they would be okay with wearing a dress like the bridesmaids. they said they weren’t sure how they would feel and they’d let me know later on. however we had a deadline to order the dresses, so i went ahead and ordered them one. i also have a suit for them, because while i’d rather they wear a dress to match, i don’t want them to be uncomfortable at all, which i made clear to them. apparently this was the wrong move, they’re completely pissed, as are my parents and brother. they’re telling me i shouldn’t have ordered either and should have let my sister decide closer to the wedding and get their own stuff, even though that means they likely wouldn’t have the matching outfit for whichever they chose. i don’t understand why it was so wrong, since they now have a suit and a dress ready and can pick day of, but they are now saying they aren’t sure they even want to be there, since i’m making decisions for them and all that. aita? one more last

text: aita for telling my flatmates i'm leaving but convincing the owner to kick them out i (23m) have been sharing a flat with alice (f19) and bob (m19) for about two years. i was in the flat before sorted by themselves. they were 18 years old when they came to the big city, both only childs. ​ but soon they stopped "giving a shit" about stuff around the house. cleaning, shopping and finances. their parents still pay for rent and their share of utilities so that's not an issue, but i have to take care of all the other stuff. this has been causing me stress. i avoid comming home until late so i wont't have to deal with the mess. i have asked them to please try to clean after themselves and be more careful, to no avail. i am not a clean freak but overnight dirty dishes do me in. ​ now the other week the flat owner called me as our contract is about to end and he wants to know if we'll stay. i told him i would first speak to my flatmates. i went to one of them (alice) and told her that it was "either them or me" but somebody would have to leave. she was totally unresponsive and stared at me blankly so i just said, alright, "i'm leaving". i said "i can't keep living like this". she left my room without a word and that was all i heard about that. ​ i was about to start looking for a new flat, but a third friend charlie who also knows alice and bob message me three days ago telling me that he heard on good account that alice and bob were celebrating with joy my leaving. quoting "that autist is finally out of this flat" and had started auctioning my room to their friends. this has not sat well with me, especially the insults. alice and bob don't know

text: aita for agreeing to puppy sit my friends dog? my friend toby is going out of state for his cousins funeral. he asked me to puppy sit for him. i excitedly said yes, and then remembered i had to ask my roommate (sara) and told him so. my roommate/best friend said yes she would love to help and puppy sit. i told him we would love to have the puppy and he dropped her (the puppy, carly) off today. since sara said she would help, and she and toby both know i work (we’re in college), i let him know that it would just be sara there when he dropped carly off. sara said this was fine and i left for work. being a puppy, carly is very excited to meet new people and has been a little rowdy. i work on saturday as well, and sara called her mom complaining that carly was too much. sara’s mom said that i was an asshole for volunteering to puppy sit when i knew i had to work. sara agreed. aita? sara also agreed to help, we’re both friends with toby. i didn’t volunteer, toby asked and while i did say yes without asking her originally, i asked to make sure it was okay with her. she very excitedly agreed, it wasn’t a “oh i guess so” kind of thing. edit 2: i also just want to clarify that sara said she wanted to help puppy sit, not just that she would be okay with me puppy sitting. she also knew that i would be working.

text: aita for expecting someone to try before asking for help? hi all, i had an argument and i want to know if y'all agree with me. my dad has a lot of health issues, especially for a 57 year old man. due to him being a very heavy drinker (i think alcoholic, but undiagnosed) and a smoker of almost 40 years, he has had breathing and heart issues. obviously this puts him at risk in the current health crisis and so he has been unable to go to work. he has a tablet (which he uses for gaming, news and music) and a phone (rarely uses and doesn't bring it with him), so he's not a total technophobe. he has a decent grasp of technology, especially for his age. because he is technically unemployed, he asked me to help him apply for benefits, fine. i ask him what part he needs help with and he tells me he doesn't know the process. i tell him he can either look on the official welfare website for our country for details of the application, or he can visit the welfare office, speak to an agent and get the application forms while he's there (which is about 5 minutes walk from his house). he gets snappy and reminds me that i would help him, but i reply telling him that i will help, but not do it for him. he loses it and tells me it doesn't matter and he will just not receive benefits because i'm not helping enough, that's the end of that for like a week. the next week, he asks again to help, i tell him the same thing as before. i repeated my answer about going to the welfare centre or looking on his tablet. he loses it again and tells me i have to do it. i ask whether he tried anything to do with the application, he says "i don't have a computer so i can't". i told him i'm not completing the application process for him because (1) he hasn't even tried to do it himself and (2) i'm pretty sure completing forms in someone elses name is illegal. after 2 days of him talking to himself, cursing me out and insulting me whenever i was within earshot so i could hear, i did everything bar signing the forms, just to shut him up. ​

text: aita for "humble bragging" about my grades? i am in a masters program at a university with a cohort consisting of 15 other people. recently we got our grades back for a presentation assignment in which many people got a large part of their points deducted as they did not stick to the rubric. i stuck to the grading rubric and made sure i answered all the questions and stuck to the 5-minute presentation time allowance. my cohort and i have a group chat and many of them complained that their grades were far lower than expected and did not reflect the amount of time dedicated to completion. curious, i asked them what the grades were and they were mostly b to c+. i got an a on the assignment and i told them that i got an a because i followed the rubric which clearly outlined the requirements and i only spent about 2 hours working on it. i said that i thought many people went overboard on the amount of irrelevant content and wasted a lot of time because of it. i got a private message from one of my classmates saying i shouldn't humble brag about my grades and that i didn't know what other people were going through in their personal lives. my intention was to let them know that they should follow the rubric (its not rocket science) and their grade will reflect how closely their work aligned with the philosophy behind assignment. i don't think i was humble bragging and i think people should take personal responsibility rather than accuse the professor of grading unfairly. also its a masters program, the grades don't matter. anyways, a group of them created a petition to re-grade the assignment and i refused to sign it. now i'm being ostracized and although i don't have any evidence for them creating a new group without me, i think that happened because the group chat is nearly dead when it had been lively before.
label: YTA

text: aita for not stopping my 4-year-old from trying to repeatedly facetime his dad while he was in a meeting? a few months ago, my ex was on a business trip to australia. while he was there, my son wanted to call him and i told him he had to wait because his daddy was sleeping. he started crying because i wouldn’t let him speak to his dad and when we finally did call him, he told my ex that i said no to calling him. my ex was furious with me and made a big show of buying our son an ipad and teaching him how to facetime him when he got back since he travels a lot and couldn’t trust me to keep communication open between him and our son. he warned me not to stop our son from contacting him ever again, which is why yesterday i didn’t stop our son from repeatedly trying to facetime his dad despite knowing he was in an important meeting. my ex thought something happened so ended his meeting early to call our son back. after he spoke to him, he called me and got angry at me for not telling our son not to call him until after his meeting. aita?
label: NTA

text: aita for yelling at security who was discriminating against us for being poor and not thin? i don't even know what happened but people are telling me i'm an ah. reddit, please help me decode or figure this out! recently, i became good friends with earl, whose father happens to be someone who's well established and respected in my girlfriend's field, which is notoriously hard to break into. earl invited us to his dad's birthday party, so my gf could try to make some connections. the dress code was white tie, so i bought a new white tie, we dressed in our finest and when we got to the party, security wouldn't let us in! he wouldn't even check the list, kept saying we weren't dressed right, but wouldn't say much more, just that the dress code is white tie. it was the wiredest thing, he kept trying to say that i needed a white tie, and i'd point to my white tie. i guess it's not the purest white, but anyone would tell you that it was white! it was ridiculous, two grown men arguing about colour. eventually, i said i'd leave, but let my girlfriend in because this was important to her. but no, apparently she wasn't dressed properly. i tried pushing a bit more, and eventually, he said that her dress was too short. but it wasn't, she can go to church in that, and none one would mind! eventually, someone came to see what the commotion was and got earl. earl told him i was his guest, but he said he's need the dad's permission and wouldn't be here for another hour. we didn't want to wait around so we left. i met up earl a bit later who apologized for the confusion but told me that next time i should just call him instead of being an ah to the security. i didn't know what to say, so i just apologized. when i told my mom what happened, she just chewed me for how what i did, that i'm an ah for perpetuating stereotypes. but i knew we were being discriminated against! i'm poor, i look poor. you can put me in a suit and i still look poor! and my gf thinks the comment about her dress is because she's larger, she gets similar comments all the time if she's wearing anything shorter than
label: YTA

text: aita for acting cold towards a guy that wants to be friends with me? so i used to live in a place that was close to a bus stop that i catch for every reason of travel (go to uni, work, etc). im a very introverted and not very social guy and that i wouldn't talk to anyone in that bus stop unless they asked for what time the is or when the bus would come. there was this guy that i would bump to sometimes and he would talk to me. every time i catch the bus with him he would tell me about his life and his routines, in which i respond with short opinions and small talks, basically giving off that im just conversing and not really wanting to be more than bus mates. it was ok at first but then he started to get a little creepy. he would tell me to sit next to him even though the bus is empty, when im with my mum on the bus (i would sit next to her) and he would stand next to us to talk about the same thing he always talks about even though the bus is empty, and at times inviting me to go eat out with him cause he's a lonely man (he's at least 10 to 15 years older than me). the creepiest he's done is when i was heading to work wearing a white textured shirt and him randomly touching my arm to which i nudge his hand away, and he would do it again seconds later and i nudged again and told him to stop. he did, but then continued to just talk about the same things over and over. the breaking point for me was when mum and i were eating in our local shopping centre and i saw this guy walked to us then comes to our table and just invited himself, and sat himself down at our table. i dont know about you guys, but i find it extremely rude to just invite yourself without asking if they are fine with you joining them. when he did this i was pissed, like ready to throw hands pissed and i didnt hide the fact that i was. but since my mum was there and i didnt want to embarrass her, i just finished up what i was eating
label: NTA
